[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2016-05-23
[TITLE]: US President Obama welcomed at Presidential Palace
[METADATA/INTRO]: This morning, the welcome ceremony to US President Barack Obama took place at the Presidential Palace, as the first official activity of Obama’s visit to Vietnam. US President Obama and National Assembly Chair Nguyen Thi Kim Ngan feed fish at...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: This morning, the welcome ceremony to US President Barack Obama took place at the Presidential Palace, as the first official activity of Obama’s visit to Vietnam. 
 US President Obama and National Assembly Chair Nguyen Thi Kim Ngan feed fish at the pond near the house-on-stilts of President Ho Chi Minh. 
 The talks between the Vietnamese and US presidents after the official welcome ceremony at the Presidential Palace this morning. Photo: Pham Hai 
 President Tran Dai Quang and President Barack Obama at the welcome ceremony. 
 President Tran Dai Quang and President Barack Obama review the guards of honor. 
 Vietnamese and American Foreign Ministers.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: The President of Vietnam and the King of Belgium visited Thang Long Imperial Citadel.
[METADATA/INTRO]: This is a complex of historical relics associated with the Thang Long Imperial Citadel of Hanoi. The structures were built by various emperors over different historical periods. In 2010, the World Heritage Committee adopted a resolution recognizing the central area...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Earlier, following the official welcoming ceremony, Mrs. Nguyen Thi Minh Nguyet and Queen Mathilde visited the Ho Chi Minh Memorial Area at the Presidential Palace. 
 The Queen of Belgium was given a presentation about the stilt house where President Ho Chi Minh once stayed and received international guests. 
 After the tour, the First Lady and the Queen fed the fish in President Ho Chi Minh's fish pond. Walking along the cool, green mango tree-lined path, First Lady Nguyen Thi Minh Nguyet gave Queen Mathilde a brief introduction to the historical site complex. 
 First Lady Nguyen Thi Minh Nguyet and Queen Mathilde walked from the Presidential Palace to the stilt house.

[TITLE]: President Obama visits Vietnam

[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: NEWSBarack ObamaAdd TopicPresident Obama visits VietnamUpdated May 23, 2016, 1:29 p.m. ETPresident Obama shakes hands with local residents on a street in Hanoi on May 23, 2016.European Pressphoto AgencyChildren wave Vietnamese and U.S. flags as President Obama arrives at the presidential palace to meet his Vietnamese counterpart (not pictured) in Hanoi on May 23, 2016.Hoang Dinh Nam, AFP/Getty ImagesA man carries his daughter near Vietnam's presidential palace as President Obama begins his three-day visit to Vietnam on May 23, 2016, in Hanoi.Linh Pham, Getty ImagesVietnamese take photos with their smartphones as President Obama's motorcade drives into Hanoi on May 23, 2016.Jim Watson, AFP/Getty ImagesRay Corner, president and CEO of Boeing Commercial Airplanes, seated at left, and Vietjet CEO Nguyen Thi Phuong Thao, seated at right, sign a contract for the purchase of 100 Boeing aircraft as President Obama and his Vietnamese counterpart Tran Dai Quang, center right, look on at the presidential palace in Hanoi on May 23, 2016.Kham, AFP/Getty ImagesPresident Obama walks with Vietnam's National Assembly Chairwoman Nguyen Thi Kim Ngan, center left, and National Security Adviser Susan Rice, center right, during his visit to the presidential palace in Hanoi on May 23, 2016.Jim Watson, AFP/Getty ImagesFeatured Weekly Ad
